17 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strikes, Feb. 16, 2026 SOUTHCOM 17 Feb 2026 -- Late on Feb. 16, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command, Marine Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted three lethal kinetic strikes on three vessels operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • State Department: No 'Gentleman's Agreement' Between US, Russia On Expired Nuclear Treaty RFE/RL 17 Feb 2026 -- A senior US State Department official has flatly rejected suggestions that Washington and Moscow are informally continuing to observe the limits of the now-expired, nuclear-weapon-limiting New START treaty, saying there is no "gentlemen's agreement" in place.
  • US to Return to Nuclear Tests on Equal Basis, Without Atmospheric Explosions - State Dept. Sputnik 17 Feb 2026 -- The United States will return to nuclear testing on an equal basis without massive atmospheric explosions, Assistant Secretary of State for Arms Control and Nonproliferation Christopher Yeaw said on Tuesday.

  • Delivering deterrence: Sentinel restructure to complete in 2026; initial capability timeline set USSTRATCOM 17 Feb 2026 -- In direct response to the 2026 National Defense Strategy's call for urgency and speed, the U.S. Air Force rolled out its plan for the LGM-35A Sentinel program, which will deliver the nation's next-generation, modernized, land-based nuclear deterrent to America's warfighters. Leveraging considerable progress over the last 12-18 months, program officials are executing a transformed acquisition strategy paving the way to complete the restructure and achieve a Milestone B decision by the end of 2026, while delivering an initial capability targeted for the early 2030s.
  • DOW's New Chief Information Officer Seeks to Put the Warfighter First DoD News 17 Feb 2026 -- The Pentagon's newest chief information officer, Kirsten Davies, gathered her department's senior leaders and staff together at the War Department's Mark Center in Alexandria, Virginia, Feb. 10, where she laid out a vision for her office that focuses strongly on supporting America's warfighters.
  • JIATF-401 Expands Counter-Drone Training, Bolsters Homeland Defense DoD News 17 Feb 2026 -- As small unmanned aircraft systems become cheaper, more accessible and increasingly weaponized, Joint Interagency Task Force 401 is expanding its efforts to strengthen the military's ability to counter the growing threat at installations in the United States and abroad.
  • Training for the Unthinkable: Task Force 2 Validates Domestic Response Readiness DoD News 17 Feb 2026 -- Soldiers from units across the force converged at Fort Bragg, North Carolina, Feb. 10-12, to validate their readiness as part of Task Force 2 under the Defense Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear and Explosives Response Force.
  • Marines Bring Amphibious Vehicles Back to Life With One Simple, Critical Component DoD News 17 Feb 2026 -- When a shortage of critical single-use consumable suspension washers resulted in multiple amphibious combat vehicles being taken out of service, Marines assigned to the Fabrication Platoon, 2nd Maintenance Battalion, at Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune, North Carolina, stepped in, turning a potentially yearlong delay into a one-hour fix.
  • Coast Guard Cutter Mohawk returns home from 60-day patrol after escorting two oil tankers in the Caribbean Sea, Gulf of America US Coast Guard 17 Feb 2026 -- The crew of Coast Guard Cutter Mohawk returned to their homeport in Key West, Saturday, after a 60-day patrol in the Caribbean Sea and Gulf of America, where crew members boarded and escorted two sanctioned oil tankers.
  • USS Farragut Arrives in Mobile, AL to Celebrate Tradition, Service, and Community US Navy 17 Feb 2026 -- During the visit, Farragut will participate in a variety of events in the community, including marching in Mobile's historic Floral Parade.
  • Former Army colonel sentenced 24 months for unauthorized disclosure of classified information Air Force Office of Special Investigations 17 Feb 2026 -- A retired U.S. Army colonel and former civilian employee assigned to United States Central Command was sentenced 24 months in federal prison for the unauthorized disclosure of classified information Feb. 11.

  • Coast Guard addresses recent media reporting US Coast Guard 17 Feb 2026 -- "A recent media report about the U.S. Coast Guard contained numerous inaccuracies about the Service. Among those, the allegation that the Secretary of Homeland Security directed the Acting Commandant to divert a C-130 long range aircraft from a search for a missing U.S. Coast Guard crewmember who went overboard from a U.S. Coast Guard cutter at sea is categorically false."

  • North Atlantic Council meets with the NATO Parliamentary Assembly, reaffirms transatlantic bond NATO 17 Feb 2026 -- On Monday (16 February 2026) NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska welcomed members of the NATO Parliamentary Assembly (NPA) to NATO Headquarters in Brussels.

  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on February 9 - 15, 2026 Lithuania MND 17 Feb 2026

  • Russian Navy Best Guarantor of Safety of Ships Amid West's Attacks - Presidential Aide Sputnik 17 Feb 2026 -- The Russian navy is the best guarantor of safety of Russian merchant vessels amid Western attacks, Russian presidential aide and chairman of the Russian Marine Board Nikolai Patrushev said.

  • AI solutions against Shahed drones, a 'drone wall', and autonomous defence systems - one year of JATEC Ukraine MOD 17 Feb 2026
  • Over 7,000 missions in January: Ukraine expands deployment of ground robotic systems Ukraine MOD 17 Feb 2026

  • Australia, the Philippines and the United States conduct maritime cooperative activity Australia DOD 17 Feb 2026 -- Australia, the Philippines and the United States have conducted a multilateral Maritime Cooperative Activity (MCA) within the Philippines' Exclusive Economic Zone.
  • Mission-ready for Exercise Red Flag Nellis Australia DOD 17 Feb 2026 -- While fighter jets roar overhead and complex air battles unfold across the Nevada desert, much of the success of Exercise Red Flag Nellis 26-1 depends on work taking place far from the spotlight.
  • New names, same missions Australia DOD 17 Feb 2026 -- January 1 was not just the start of a new year, it was also the establishment of 94 Wing and 294 Squadron, formally known as Health Services Wing (HSW) and Health Operational Conversion Unit (HOCU) respectively.

  • New Uncrewed Aerial System a game changer for RNZAF Base Ohakea New Zealand Defence Force 17 Feb 2026 -- A high-tech Uncrewed Aerial System, Skydio X10, recently acquired by Royal New Zealand Air Force (RNZAF) Base Ohakea can be used for surveillance, building and aircraft inspections and emergency situations, marking a shift in capability for the base.

  • Prime Minister & French President virtually inaugurate Final Assembly Line of H-125 Light Utility Helicopter at Vemagal, Karnataka India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and French President Mr Emmanuel Macron, on February 17, 2026, virtually inaugurated, from Mumbai, Tata Advanced Systems Final Assembly Line of the Airbus H-125 Light Utility Helicopter, located at Vemagal, Karnataka
  • Raksha Mantri & his French counterpart co-chair 6th Annual Defence Dialogue in Bengaluru India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh and Minister of the Armed Forces and Veterans Affairs of France Ms Catherine Vautrin co-chaired the 6th India-France Annual Defence Dialogue in Bengaluru, Karnataka on February 17, 2026
  • Visit of high-level delegation of Tanzania Peoples' Defence Forces to India India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- A high-level delegation of the Tanzania Peoples' Defence Forces (TPDF) is on a three-day visit to India from February 17-19, 2026
  • Indian Coast Guard and Gujarat ATS intercept speed boat with 203 kg Crystalline Drug-like substance India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- In a swift and well-coordinated overnight operation on February 16, 2026, the Indian Coast Guard (ICG), in close coordination with the Gujarat Anti-Terrorism Squad (ATS), intercepted a speed boat carrying approx
  • INDIAN NAVY TO CONDUCT GOA MARITIME CONCLAVE 2026 AT NAVAL WAR COLLEGE, GOA India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- The fifth edition of Goa Maritime Conclave (GMC-26) is scheduled to be held at the Naval War College, Goa, on 21 Feb 2026.
  • VICE ADMIRAL KK NAYYAR MEMORIAL LECTURE 2026 India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- The 2026 edition of the Vice Admiral KK Nayyar Memorial Lecture was held on 16 Feb 2026, marking the 21st Foundation Day of the National Maritime Foundation (NMF), New Delhi
  • LAUNCH OF FIRST CADET TRAINING SHIP - KRISHNA AT M/s L&T, KATTUPALLI, CHENNAI India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- Yard 18003 (Krishna), the first of the three Cadet Training Ships (CTS) under construction at M/s L&T, Kattupalli (Chennai), has been launched on 16 Feb 2026.

  • Warships can be sent to the seabed, Leader warns in response to Trump's threats Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- The Leader of the Islamic Revolution has responded to repeated US military threats against Iran, saying that the weapons that can sink the American carriers are "more dangerous" than the warships.
  • IRGC Navy holds drills in Strait of Hormuz for second day Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- The naval forces of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) are conducting the second day of its naval drills in the Strait of Hormuz, showcasing 24-hour "intelligence dominance" over the strategically vital waterway.
  • Iran Ready to Close Hormuz Strait, ASAP If Needed - IRGC Naval Commander Sputnik 17 Feb 2026 -- Iran is ready to close the Strait of Hormuz, if necessary, as soon as possible, Commander of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy Rear Adm. Alireza Tangsiri said on Tuesday.
  • Strait of Hormuz under 24-hour surveillance: IRGC navy chief IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- The commander of the naval force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps has informed about the round-the-clock surveillance by the IRGC intelligence unit over the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most important oil transit route linking the Persian Gulf to the Indian Ocean.

  • Prime Minister Carney announces the launch of Canada's first Defence Industrial Strategy Prime Minister of Canada 17 Feb 2026
  • Prime Minister Carney launches Canada's first Defence Industrial Strategy to strengthen security, create prosperity, and reinforce strategic autonomy Prime Minister of Canada 17 Feb 2026 -- To protect Canada's sovereignty, build our prosperity, and strengthen our strategic autonomy, the Canadian government must change how we invest in defence. Canada's defence procurement has long been too complicated, too slow, and too reliant on international suppliers, limiting the growth of our defence industries

  • Japan-U.S. Extended Deterrence Dialogue Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 17 Feb 2026

  • Switzerland weighs European option as Patriot delivery stalls Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Switzerland is reassessing its air defense plans after the United States postponed delivery of Patriot missile systems, with a European alternative now under consideration.

  • European and world leaders discussed security and defence policy issues in Munich Czech Republic Ministry of Defence & Armed Forces 17 Feb 2026 -- With attendance of over 60 Heads of State and Governments from around the globe, dozens of Presidents and Prime Ministers, the 62nd Munich Security Conference was held last week.

  • EDA project develops technology for underwater drones to move in swarms European Defence Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- The European Defence Agency (EDA) has concluded the second stage of its Swarm of Biomimetic Underwater Vehicles (SABUVIS II) project after four years, preparing for a shift in naval operations beneath the seas.

  • Economic governance: Council approves flexibility for Austria to increase defence spending EC/CEU 17 Feb 2026 -- The Council today activated the national escape clause (NEC) under the stability and growth pact (SGP) for Austria. The measure will help Austria transition to higher defence spending at national level without putting its debt sustainability at risk.

  • The President at the LTG Forum: Military mobility is a prerequisite for strengthening European security President of the Republic of Lithuania 17 Feb 2026 -- On Tuesday, Gitanas Nausėda welcomed the participants of the LTG Forum on Military Mobility and Resilience in Vilnius.

  • US Congress delegation, on official visit to the Ministry of National Defence Romania - Ministry of National Defence 17 Feb 2026 -- On Monday, February 16, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, received the visit of a United States Congress delegation, led by Senator Jeanne Shaheen.
  • Defence Minister's meeting with the new Ambassador of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan in Romania Romania - Ministry of National Defence 17 Feb 2026 -- On Tuesday, February 17, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, received, at the MoND headquarters, a presentation visit by the new Ambassador of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan to Romania, H.E. Ilyas Mehmood Nizami.

  • Joint Statement on the Fourth Philippines-United States Bilateral Maritime Dialogue Philippines DFA 17 Feb 2026 -- The following statement was jointly released by the Philippines and the United States on the occasion of the successful conclusion of the fourth Philippines-United States Bilateral Maritime Dialogue in Manila.
  • PCG acquires US King Air planes to boost search, rescue capacity Philippine News Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) formally began the acquisition of three Beechcraft King Air turboprop airplanes from the United States to boost the country's maritime domain awareness (MDA) and search and rescue (SAR) capabilities.
  • PH, US reaffirm alliance, vow to defend freedom of navigation Philippine News Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- The Philippines and the United States have reaffirmed their decades-long alliance, and committed to defend freedom of navigation and lawful use of the seas amid rising tensions in the South China Sea.
  • Air defense component eyed for PH Army's artillery regiment Philippine News Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- Philippine Army (PA) chief Lt. Gen. Antonio Nafarrete said the Army Artillery Regiment will be reconfigured into an Artillery Command and will feature an air defense capability.
  • Australian frigate's visit showcases growing naval ties with PH Philippine News Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) on Tuesday said the just-concluded visit of the Royal Australian Navy's HMAS Toowoomba (FFH-156) to the country reflects the growing naval partnership and commitment of the Philippines and Australia in safeguarding the maritime domain.
  • Outgoing Wescom chief feted for expanding maritime domain awareness Philippine News Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) chief Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr. has hailed outgoing Western Command (Wescom) head Vice Adm. Alfonso Torres Jr. for his leadership that allowed the unit to expand its maritime domain awareness capabilities in its area of operations, which includes the West Philippine Sea (WPS).
  • PH, Australia, US hold 14th joint maritime drill in WPS Philippine News Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- Naval and air units from the Philippines, Australia, and the United States completed the 14th multilateral maritime cooperative activity (MMCA) in the West Philippine Sea (WPS), the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said on Tuesday.

  • United States and Mauritius to Hold Bilateral Security Discussions US Dept. of State 17 Feb 2026 -- From February 23-25, the United States and Mauritius will hold discussions in Port Louis led by the Department of State's Bureau of Political-Military Affairs, with participation from across the U.S. interagency.

  • 17 February 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 17 Feb 2026

  • Israel intensifies crackdown at Al-Aqsa Mosque ahead of Ramadan Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Israeli police said they will deploy heavily around the al-Aqsa Mosque throughout the holy month of Ramadan, as the Tel Aviv regime announces new restrictions at the sacred compound.

  • Hamas says it has not received deadline for disarmament as Israel threatens to reignite war Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Hamas says it has not received a 60-day deadline to surrender all its weapons as Israel threatens to reignite war in the Gaza Strip if the Palestinian resistance group refuses to disarm.

  • Ramadan in Gaza: Deprivation, high prices and the hardships of displacement UN News 17 Feb 2026 -- Palestinian Walid Al-Assi was playing with his young granddaughter this week, promising to take her to the market in Gaza City with the arrival of the holy month of Ramadan as his family had done before the Israel-Hamas war, but now circumstances had changed.
  • Humanitarian access to Gaza still insufficient for relief and rebuilding: UN development chief UN News 17 Feb 2026 -- The UN has the funds and capacity to scale up relief and reconstruction operations in Gaza but does not have the necessary access to carry out these vital tasks, Alexander De Croo, the head of the UN Development Programme (UNDP), told journalists on Tuesday.

  • Special Procedures' Coordination Committee condemns attacks against Francesca Albanese by State representatives: 'Promote justice, not disinformation' OHCHR 17 Feb 2026 -- The Coordination Committee of the Special Procedures of the Human Rights Council today condemned vicious attacks, rooted in disinformation, by several State Ministers against Francesca Albanese, the Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian territories occupied since 1967.

  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan UAE, Indonesia, Türkiye , Saudi Arabia, Qatar Condemning Israel's Illegal Decision of Designation and Occupation of Occupied West Bank as State Land and Illegal Settlement Activity Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Feb 2026 -- The Foreign Ministers of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Arab Republic of Egypt, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the United Arab Emirates, the Republic of Indonesia, the Republic of Türkiye, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and the State of Qatar strongly condemn the decision issued by Israel to designate lands in the occupied West Bank as so called "state land" and approve procedures for the registration and settlement of land ownership across extensive areas of the occupied West Bank for the first time since 1967.
  • Spanish Government strongly condemns recent decision by Israeli Government to approve registration of large West Bank areas as 'State property' Spain MFA 17 Feb 2026 -- The Spanish Government strongly condemns the recent decision by the Israeli Government to approve the registration of large areas of the West Bank as 'State property'.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Ukraine, Burkina Faso, Madagascar UNOCHA 17 Feb 2026

  • Maternal deaths spike during war and instability, new report warns UN News 17 Feb 2026 -- Nearly two thirds of all maternal deaths worldwide occur in countries marked by conflict or fragility, according to a report released on Tuesday by the World Health Organization (WHO) and partners.

  • Chinese military conducts combat readiness patrols in South China Sea Global Times 17 Feb 2026 -- The Chinese military conducted naval and air combat readiness patrols in the territorial waters of the South China Sea from Sunday to Monday, a spokesperson said on Tuesday.

  • Collaboration between the European Union and the Saudi Development and Reconstruction Program for Yemen European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 Feb 2026 -- Today, the European Union (EU) and the Saudi Development and Reconstruction Programme for Yemen (SDRPY) joined efforts to improve livelihood conditions for Yemenis, underscoring the importance of investing in economic recovery and reforms, creating jobs, reinforcing the state and fostering societal resilience, including in areas such as food security, agriculture, electricity as well as water, sanitation and hygiene.

  • On 16 February 2026, a cowardly terrorist attack was attempted on Security Forces and Law Enforcement Agencies Joint Check Post in Bajaur District by Khwarij belonging to Indian Proxy, Fitna al Khwarij. ISPR Pakistan 17 Feb 2026

  • Sudan crisis: UN agency launches $1.6 billion appeal to support refugees in seven countries UN News 17 Feb 2026 -- As the war in Sudan approaches a fourth year, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, and 123 partners appealed on Tuesday for $1.6 billion to support millions of people forced to flee the country in pursuit of safety.
  • As Sudan war approaches fourth year, humanitarian agencies appeal for support to refugees UNHCR 17 Feb 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by Mamadou Dian Balde, UNHCR's Regional Director for Eastern and Southern Africa - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.

  • EU announces over €81 million in humanitarian aid as conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo pushes Great Lakes crisis to new extremes European Commission 17 Feb 2026 -- The escalation of conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is driving mass displacement.

  • Hague War Crimes Trial Casts Shadow Over Kosovo's 18th Birthday Celebrations RFE/RL 17 Feb 2026 -- Kosovo is turning 18, but this year the anniversary is marked by protests as well as celebrations.

News Reports

  • Arrest of Mongolian 'spy' shows PH firm on protecting nat'l security Philippine News Agency 17 Feb 2026 -- National Security Adviser (NSA) Eduardo Año on Tuesday said the government is committed to safeguarding Philippine national security, as highlighted by the arrest of a foreigner in Zambales for espionage-linked activities.

  • DHS Announces Historic Next Step in Border Wall Project DHS 17 Feb 2026 -- The United States Department of Homeland Security today announced that, after an extremely competitive bidding process and engagement with dozens of companies, the highly respected Parsons Government Services Inc. will oversee and manage the completion of border wall construction.

  • US secretly deports migrants to Cameroon despite court protections Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- The United States has quietly deported nine migrants to Cameroon under a secret arrangement, including individuals who had already been granted legal protection by US courts prohibiting their return.

  • Transferred Mexican National Sentenced for Role in Large Scale International Cocaine Trafficking Offense US Dept. of Justice 17 Feb 2026 -- A Mexican national was sentenced today to 10 years in prison for his role in a conspiracy to import approximately 1,900 kilograms of cocaine into the United States.

  • Security Council Press Statement on Terrorist Attack in Nigeria UN Security Council 17 Feb 2026

  • Global commitment on display as countries negotiate key annex to the Pandemic Agreement WHO 17 Feb 2026

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Ukraine's 2027 EU Dream And What Brussels Wants From Moscow RFE/RL 17 Feb 2026

  • Joint likeminded statement on the occasion of the second anniversary of Alexei Navalny`s death on 16 February 2024 Australia DFAT 17 Feb 2026 -- On the second commemoration of Alexei Navalny's death in a Russian penal colony, we once again express our deepest condolences to his family. We underline that the Russian authorities remain solely responsible

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's working lunch with the ambassadors of BRICS member and partner countries Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Feb 2026

  • Wang Yi Meets with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic Petr Macinka PRC MofA 17 Feb 2026 -- On February 14, 2026 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic Petr Macinka at the latter's request on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.

  • U.S., Philippines reiterate importance of Taiwan Strait peace CNA 17 Feb 2026 -- The United States and the Philippines have reaffirmed the importance of regional security in the Taiwan Strait following the 12th Philippines-United States Bilateral Strategic Dialogue in Manila Monday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 17 Feb 2026

  • Plan for Building 50 000 Flats in Pyongyang, Decided by Eighth Congress of WPK, Successfully Over-fulfilled Inauguration Ceremony of 10 000 Flats at Fourth Stage in Hwasong Area Held with Splendor KCNA 17 Feb 2026 -- The construction of 50 000 flats in Pyongyang, powerfully carried on as the top-priority task of the historic Eighth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea, has been successfully concluded to be an epoch-making success in the new era of the comprehensive national rejuvenation.
  • Decree of SPA Standing Committee of DPRK Issued KCNA 17 Feb 2026 -- State commendations were given to soldier-builders, youth shock brigade members and commanding officers of the People's Army and officials who performed labor feats in successfully building the Sinuiju Combined Greenhouse Farm.

  • No Agreement As Iran, US Wrap Up Second Round Of Talks In Geneva RFE/RL 17 Feb 2026 -- A second round of indirect talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's nuclear program concluded in Geneva, Switzerland, on February 17 without an agreement.
  • Second Round of US-Iran Nuclear Talks Ends in Geneva - Reports Sputnik 17 Feb 2026 -- The second round of negotiations between Washington and Tehran on the Iranian nuclear issue ended in Geneva on Tuesday, Axios reported, citing a US official.
  • Latest round of indirect Iran-US nuclear talks concludes in Geneva Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- The second round of indirect nuclear negotiations between Iran and the United States at the Omani consulate general in Geneva, Switzerland, has concluded.
  • Iran FM: 'Good progress' made in latest round of indirect talks with US, 'clear path' ahead Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has announced that the latest round of negotiations on sanctions removal with the United States concluded with an agreement on a set of guiding principles that will shape future discussions toward a potential deal.
  • Araghchi: 'Good progress' made in indirect talks with US IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says "good progress" was made during the indirect talks between Tehran and Washington in Geneva compared to the previous session.
  • Second round of Iran-US talks wraps up in Geneva IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- The second round of indirect nuclear negotiations between Tehran and Washington has come to an end, according to Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei.
  • Iran conveys nuclear, sanctions positions to US in indirect Geneva talks: Official IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei says that Tehran's positions on the nuclear issue, sanctions relief, and the framework for any potential understanding have been conveyed to the US side through diplomatic channels.
  • 'I'll Be Involved in Those Talks Indirectly' — Trump on US-Iran Talks in Geneva Sputnik 17 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said he will be involved "indirectly" in talks with Iran in Geneva on Tuesday, calling them "very important."
    • close to the Iranian negotiating team.
  • Trump's indirect role in talks US internal matter: Iranian sources IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- Iran views any indirect involvement by US President Donald Trump in nuclear talks is a matter for Washington, not Tehran, according to sources
  • Iran's right to use peaceful nuclear energy non-negotiable, FM says IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has emphasized that Iran's right to use peaceful nuclear energy is inherent, non-negotiable, and legally binding.
  • Exclusive | FM spokesperson says 'serious' Iran-US talks underway in Geneva IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei has said that the ongoing talks between Iran and the United States in Geneva are serious and have delved into the specifics regarding the removal of sanctions and nuclear discussions.
  • Iran-US resume indirect talks IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- The indirect Iran-US talks has started, says the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA) correspondent in Geneva, adding that the talks had practically began on Monday with a round of preliminary discussions between Iran and Oman, during which Iran's viewpoints were explained.
  • Iran warns about consequences of normalizing violations of intl. rules Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned about the consequences of normalizing violations of international rules and norms.
  • Iran to pursue strong defense, careful diplomacy during indirect talks with US: Deputy FM Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Deputy Foreign Minister Majid Takht-Ravanchi says Iran must remain alert and maintain maximum defensive capability to prevent the American side from attempting to extract concessions through pressure.
  • Iran in no way seeking nuclear weapons: Pezeshkian Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has once again reiterated that the country is not seeking nuclear weapons in any way or form.
  • Iran's right to nuclear enrichment non-negotiable, Araghchi tells disarmament conference Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has declared that Iran's right to peaceful nuclear energy is "inherent and non-negotiable," while simultaneously confirming that a "new window of opportunity" has opened for diplomacy with the United States.
  • Iranian, Swiss FMs review latest developments in nuclear talks IRNA 17 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met his Swiss counterpart Ignazio Cassis on Tuesday to discuss the latest status of indirect nuclear negotiations between Iran and the United States.
  • Oman: Iran-US indirect talks in Geneva achieve tangible progress Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- The Omani Ministry of Foreign Affairs says the latest round of indirect talks between the Islamic Republic of Iran and the United States in Geneva has concluded with tangible progress, paving the way for future discussions.
  • Following Iran Nuclear Talks, China's Support For Tehran Returns To The Spotlight RFE/RL 17 Feb 2026 -- As the latest nuclear talks ended in Geneva on February 17, the United States is looking at tightening pressure on Iran's discounted crude exports to China, seen as central to Tehran's economic survival.

  • US threatens Iraq with sanctions targeting key institutions if Maliki returns as PM: Report Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Washington has reportedly sent a message to Iraq's main Shia alliance, which holds a parliamentary majority, warning of potential US sanctions should the bloc proceed with the nomination of Nouri al-Maliki for the Arab country's next prime minister.

  • India - France Joint Statement. Élysée - President of France 17 Feb 2026 -- At the invitation of Hon'ble Prime Minister of India, Shri Narendra Modi, President of the French Republic H.E. Mr. Emmanuel Macron paid an official visit to India from 17 to 19 February 2026 and participated in the Artificial Intelligence Impact Summit 2026.
  • List of Outcomes: Visit of the President of the French Republic to India India PIB 17 Feb 2026
  • English translation of Prime Minister's Press Statement during the Joint Press Statement with the President of France India PIB 17 Feb 2026
  • Prime Minister Welcomes French President to India, Emphasises Strengthening Bilateral Ties India PIB 17 Feb 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi extended a warm welcome to President Emmanuel Macron of France, expressing confidence that the visit will elevate India-France relations to new heights

  • Joint Statement between The Government of the Republic of Austria represented by H.E. Mr. Christian Stocker, Federal Chancellor of the Republic of Austria and The Islamic Republic of Pakistan represented by H.E. Mr. Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Feb 2026 -- The Federal Chancellor of the Republic of Austria, Mr. Christian Stocker, received the Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic Pakistan, Mr. Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif, for an official visit on 16 February 2026. The visit marked the 70th anniversary of the establishment of bilateral relations between Austria and Pakistan in 1956.

  • Statement by Cuba's Foreign Minister, Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla, through his X account Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Feb 2026 -- The measure adopted by the #US government on January 29, based on the fallacy of an alleged threat to the US national security, whereby it threatens to impose punitive tariffs on those countries that export fuel to #Cuba, poses a challenge of huge proportions to all States.
  • 'Not Necessary': Trump Rules Out 'Maduro-Style' Operation in Cuba Sputnik 17 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said a US operation against Cuba, similar to the kidnapping of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, is unnecessary.

  • The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session in Riyadh SPA 17 Feb 2026 -- Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet session held today in Riyadh

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna in annual foreign policy address: Estonia's goal is peace in Ukraine, but not a version of Munich or Yalta Estonia MFA 17 Feb 2026 -- Today, 17 February, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna delivered Estonia's annual foreign policy address in the Riigikogu, outlining the main priorities and objectives of Estonia's foreign policy.
  • Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna's annual foreign policy report 2026 Estonia MFA 17 Feb 2026

  • Tarique Rahman sworn in as Bangladesh's new prime minister Press TV 17 Feb 2026 -- Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) leader Tarique Rahman has been sworn in as the country's new prime minister following his party's decisive parliamentary election victory.

  • Zimbabwe: Council renews arms embargo and lifts travel bans and assets freeze EC/CEU 17 Feb 2026 -- The European Union has concluded its annual review of the restrictive measures in relation to Zimbabwe.

  • New strategy for reform cooperation with Eastern Europe, the Western Balkans and Türkiye Government Offices of Sweden 17 Feb 2026 -- The Government has adopted a new strategy for Sweden's reform cooperation with Eastern Europe, the Western Balkans and Türkiye for 2026-2030. The aim of the strategy is to strengthen the countries' reform efforts and bring them closer to the EU.

  • Top Kyrgyz Court Stops President Potentially Ruling Until 2037 RFE/RL 17 Feb 2026 -- The Constitutional Court in Kyrgyzstan has issued a ruling with implications for an ongoing political power struggle that will suit President Sadyr Japarov - while also throwing an obstacle to any long-term aim he may have to stay in power until 2037.

  • World News in Brief: Madagascar cyclone response, Gaza medical evacuations, remembering civil rights icon Jesse Jackson UN News 17 Feb 2026 -- At least 52 people have died after Tropical Cyclone Gezani made landfall on the eastern coast of Madagascar on 10 February, the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) said on Tuesday, citing the authorities.
